title: Lost in the Crowd
theme: Loneliness among young adults can develop from multiple life pressures, even when surrounded by people.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Hundreds of students move across campus. Clubs advertise events. Friends laugh between classes.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: ETHAN, 22, walks through the crowd carrying a backpack and a coffee.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: This place is packed.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Doesn't seem like a place where someone could feel lonely.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: You'd be surprised.

scenes.prompt: Watch Ethan carefully. Something feels off.

dialogue.speaker: narration
dialogue.text: The professor asks Ethan a question.

dialogue.speaker: narration
dialogue.text: Ethan stares blankly before giving a short answer.

dialogue.speaker: avery
dialogue.text: He's been like that for a while.

dialogue.speaker: avery
dialogue.text: Difficulty concentrating. That's our first clue.

closing.speaker: narration
closing.text: The lecture ends. Students file out in groups, chatting. Ethan packs his bag slowly. Alone.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Ethan's phone rings. He glances at the screen. MOM. He picks up.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: MOTHER: Are you eating properly? How's university?

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: ETHAN: Everything's fine.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: The call ends quickly.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: That didn't sound convincing.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Sometimes young adults stop talking about what they're struggling with.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Because saying 'everything's fine' is easier than explaining why it isn't.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — feeling disconnected from family support.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Several classmates gather around a table, working on an assignment.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: CLASSMATE: We're getting lunch after this. Wanna come?

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: ETHAN: Maybe next time.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: The group leaves. Ethan stays behind, staring at his notes.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: He keeps turning people down.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Notice that.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Is he just busy or is he avoiding them?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Sometimes those two things look exactly the same from the outside.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — avoiding social opportunities.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: INT. CONVENIENCE STORE — EVENING. Ethan stocks shelves. A coworker leans against the counter.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: COWORKER: Why do you always volunteer for overtime?

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: ETHAN: Keeps me busy.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Busy doing what?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Sometimes staying busy feels easier than dealing with your emotions.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: So work becomes an escape?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Yeah. If you're always doing something, you never have to sit with how you're feeling.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — overworking as a coping mechanism.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Ethan cleans his apartment. He moves a box from under his desk. A photo falls out.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Him and a girl, laughing at something. He stares at it for a moment.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Breakup?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Five months ago.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Still affects him?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Some losses take time.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Five months feels like a long time.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Depends on what the relationship meant to him. If she was also his closest connection here... losing her meant losing a lot more than just her.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — difficulty moving on after relationship loss.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Outside work. The street is quiet. Ethan leans against the wall and lights a cigarette.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Not exactly healthy.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Stress?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Sometimes people use unhealthy habits to escape difficult emotions.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Because the emotions feel worse?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Because the emotions feel like too much. And a cigarette, or a drink, or hours of gaming — they all do the same thing. They pause the feeling.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: But they don't fix it.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: No. They don't.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — unhealthy coping mechanism.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Back in the apartment. A guitar sits untouched in the corner. Football boots gather dust near the door.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Looks like he used to have hobbies.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Used to.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Good question.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: Hobbies don't just disappear.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: They do when everything starts feeling pointless. When you don't have the energy. When the things that used to bring joy... just don't anymore.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: That sounds like more than just stress.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: It usually is.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Clue added — loss of interest in enjoyable activities.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: 2:30 AM. Ethan sits alone. No work. No classes. No distractions.

lines.speaker: narration
lines.text: Just the quiet.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: I think I understand now.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Tell me.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: He's away from home. He doesn't feel connected to people around him. He's working constantly. He lost an important relationship. He's stopped doing things he enjoys. And he's using unhealthy ways to cope.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Loneliness in young adults usually isn't caused by one thing. It's often several life changes happening at the same time.

lines.speaker: player
lines.text: And none of them look obvious from the outside.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: That's the part most people miss.

lines.speaker: avery
lines.text: Let's see if you can identify what's really going on.

scenes.title: Identify the Causes
scenes.instructions: Drag each behaviour into the category that best explains it.

signals.statement: Stares blankly during lectures and gives short, distracted answers.
signals.explanation: Difficulty concentrating in class is often a sign of emotional overload affecting cognitive function.

signals.statement: Tells his mother everything is fine when it clearly isn't.
signals.explanation: Hiding struggles from family is a form of emotional withdrawal — cutting off support systems even when they're available.

signals.statement: Declines lunch with classmates and says 'maybe next time.'
signals.explanation: Repeatedly avoiding social opportunities reinforces isolation even when connections are available.

signals.statement: Volunteers for overtime shifts to stay busy.
signals.explanation: Using overwork to avoid emotions is a coping mechanism — it numbs the feeling temporarily but doesn't address the underlying cause.

signals.statement: Found a photo of his ex-girlfriend and stared at it.
signals.explanation: Unresolved grief from a breakup can be a major driver of loneliness, especially when that person was a key emotional connection.

signals.statement: Smokes outside work alone at night.
signals.explanation: Substance use as emotional escape masks difficult emotions rather than processing them.

signals.statement: Guitar and football boots sit untouched for months.
signals.explanation: Loss of interest in hobbies is a sign of emotional withdrawal — activities that once brought joy no longer feel worth the effort.

scenes.title: Chapter 2 Complete
scenes.summary: Ethan lives in a campus full of people. He has classmates, a job, a family that calls. But none of it reached him. Loneliness at 22 doesn't look like sitting alone in an empty room. It looks like saying 'everything's fine' when it isn't. It looks like volunteering for overtime. It looks like a guitar no one plays.
keyLearnings: Loneliness in young adults is often caused by multiple pressures happening simultaneously.
Overworking, substance use, and avoidance are common but unhealthy coping strategies.
Withdrawing from family and social opportunities deepens isolation over time.
Loss of interest in hobbies can be an early signal of emotional exhaustion.
Being surrounded by people does not protect against loneliness.
